novel_features.name: Mirror container to SQLite
novel_features.description: Walk an entire GTM container — every tag, trigger, variable, folder, template, client, zone, and gtag config — into a local SQLite store in one command.
novel_features.rationale: A local cross-referenced store is the precondition for every offline, diff, and graph query the GTM API and console cannot answer on their own.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first — every other command reads the mirror, and re-pulls become time-stamped snapshots for history/drift.

novel_features.name: Hygiene audit
novel_features.description: Run a battery of hygiene checks against the mirror: dead tags, orphan triggers, unused variables, paused tags, Custom HTML tags, and tags firing on All Pages.
novel_features.rationale: These checks require joining the full tag/trigger/variable graph locally, which the per-resource GTM UI and single-call API cannot do.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to gate a release in CI or sanity-check a container before publishing; typed exit code fails the build on high-severity findings.

novel_features.name: Snapshot diff
novel_features.description: Field-level diff between two snapshots: workspace vs live, version vs version, or container vs container, with stable name+type keys so renames are not add+remove.
novel_features.rationale: The GTM console has no diff of any kind; cross-container and version-to-version comparison is only possible against the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before publishing to see exactly what changed, or to compare two properties' containers.

novel_features.name: Cross-container fleet matrix
novel_features.description: One table across all pulled containers: GA4/measurement-id inventory, Consent Mode coverage, Custom HTML count, and entity counts per container.
novel_features.rationale: The GTM UI is single-container; a fleet-wide inventory only exists once many containers share one local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you manage containers across multiple properties and need a portfolio view the console can never give.

novel_features.name: Consent Mode v2 readiness
novel_features.description: Per-tag consentSettings coverage and a list of tags that fire without consent gating, classified by vendor (ads vs analytics).
novel_features.rationale: Derived from the joined tag/consent graph in the mirror — the API returns consentSettings per tag but never the rolled-up coverage view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to answer 'are we Consent Mode v2 compliant for the EEA' without clicking through every tag.

novel_features.name: Full-text search across config
novel_features.description: FTS5 search across every entity's name, type, parameters, and notes — find hardcoded GA4 ids, stray pixels, or leftover URLs across all pulled containers.
novel_features.rationale: Searching parameter blobs across all entities and containers at once requires the flattened local index, not per-resource API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to track down where a measurement id, domain, or vendor pixel is referenced before changing or removing it.

novel_features.name: Reverse-dependency blast radius
novel_features.description: Show everything that references a variable or trigger — the safe-to-delete / what-breaks-if-I-touch-this check.
novel_features.rationale: A reverse reference index across all tags and triggers is only available from the joined local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before deleting or renaming a variable or trigger to see its blast radius.

novel_features.name: Runtime fire graph
novel_features.description: Bidirectional graph walk: which tags fire on a trigger, or which triggers and variables a tag depends on.
novel_features.rationale: Resolving the trigger-tag-variable graph in both directions needs the cross-referenced store, not a single GET.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for incident debugging — 'why did this fire' or 'what fires here'.

novel_features.name: Git-friendly export
novel_features.description: Emit a deterministic flattened representation of the container for clean version-control diffs, or container-export-compatible JSON.
novel_features.rationale: Stable key ordering across a flattened entity set produces VCS-friendly diffs the GTM native export does not.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to check GTM config into git and review changes in pull requests.

narrative.headline: Mirror any GTM container into a local database, then audit, diff, and query it from the terminal — everything the GTM console can't do, read-only and safe against production.
narrative.value_prop: The Google Tag Manager console can't diff anything, can't bulk-query, can't cross-reference the tag/trigger/variable graph, and can't export to version control. This CLI pulls a container into local SQLite and turns all of that into one command: audit hygiene, diff workspace-vs-live or container-vs-container, inventory GA4 ids and Consent Mode coverage across your whole fleet, and full-text search every parameter. It is read-only by construction — the mutating endpoints are stripped, so it is safe to point at a production container.
narrative.auth_narrative: Google Tag Manager has no API key — it uses OAuth 2.0. Provide a read-scoped access token as GTM_ACCESS_TOKEN. The quickest path with gcloud: run `gcloud auth application-default login --scopes=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/tagmanager.readonly`, then `export GTM_ACCESS_TOKEN=$(gcloud auth application-default print-access-token)`. Alternatively use a service account whose email is granted Viewer access in GTM admin and mint a token for it. The CLI sends the token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and only ever needs the read-only scope.

quickstart.comment: Check the token and API reachability before anything else
Mirror the live container into the local store
Run the hygiene battery against the mirror
Find where a measurement id is referenced

troubleshoots.fix: GTM_ACCESS_TOKEN is unset or expired; mint a fresh one with gcloud auth application-default print-access-token
Re-run gcloud auth application-default login with --scopes=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/tagmanager.readonly, or grant the service account Viewer access in GTM admin
Run pull first — the query commands read the local mirror, not the live API

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 on a container you can see in the UI
troubleshoots.fix: The token's identity differs from your GTM login; confirm the token's account has access to that container

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to audit, diff, export, and query Google Tag Manager container configuration from the terminal, CI, or an agent. It is the right tool for hygiene checks before a release, comparing two containers or a workspace against live, inventorying GA4 ids and Consent Mode coverage across many containers, and full-text search over tag/trigger/variable config. It is read-only — it never creates, edits, deletes, or publishes.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to create, edit, delete, or publish tags, triggers, variables, or versions — it is read-only by design; use the GTM console or the write API for changes.
Do not use it to render or test tag firing in a browser; it reads configuration, not runtime behavior — use Google Tag Assistant for live firing tests.
Do not use it for Google Analytics report data; this is GTM container configuration, not GA4 analytics.

recipes.title: Pre-publish review
recipes.explanation: See exactly what a workspace changes relative to the published live container before publishing.

recipes.title: Consent Mode v2 audit
recipes.explanation: List every tag with its consent gating status, narrowed to just name and status for a compact agent-friendly payload.

recipes.title: Fleet GA4 inventory
recipes.explanation: Export which GA4 measurement ids appear in which containers across your whole estate as CSV.

recipes.title: Blast-radius before deleting a variable
recipes.explanation: List every tag and trigger that references a variable so you know what breaks before touching it.

recipes.title: Find stray pixels
recipes.explanation: Full-text search every entity across all pulled containers for leftover vendor pixels, narrowing the nested response to the fields that matter.

trigger_phrases: diff my GTM workspace against live
what GA4 ids are in my tag manager
what fires on this GTM trigger
